item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.6 No.22-24, 1958
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

sketch_descriptions: Top left quarter: A sketched scene showing several figures and huts in a tropical environment with palm trees, with two men conversing in the lower left corner.

Bottom left quarter: Several figures in an indoor or market environment including an older person sitting, a person with large earrings or tusks, and other figures in the background, with various bottles and objects on a table.

Right page: A crowded indoor scene with multiple figures, including a woman holding a baby, an older person with two children nearby, a man wearing a cap, and a large table piled with fruits or produce.
pages.page_summary: The page contains expressive ink sketches depicting various people and scenes from Sarawak, specifically the city of Kuching, showing both outdoor tropical settings and crowded indoor market or domestic environments.
